description: Review frameworks used to evaluate AI safety and governance risks in African healthcare AI deployment scenarios.

frameworks.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)
review_focus: Govern, map, measure, and manage AI risks
Document risk management roles and accountability
Monitor performance, robustness, and harmful impacts over time

frameworks.name: WHO Ethics and Governance of Artificial Intelligence for Health
review_focus: Protect autonomy, safety, and public interest in health AI
Require human oversight for clinical use
Ensure transparency, explainability, inclusiveness, and equity

frameworks.name: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
review_focus: Assess human rights, fairness, transparency, and accountability
Protect vulnerable groups and cultural diversity
Support governance across the AI lifecycle

frameworks.name: OECD AI Principles
review_focus: Promote inclusive growth, human-centered values, and fairness
Ensure robustness, security, safety, and accountability

frameworks.name: African Union Continental AI Strategy
review_focus: Support locally relevant, inclusive, and beneficial AI adoption
Build African AI governance capacity and digital sovereignty
Account for local infrastructure, data, workforce, and institutional context

frameworks.name: African Union Data Policy Framework
review_focus: Strengthen data governance, rights, trust, and cross-border data policy
Protect sensitive health data and community interests
Consider data stewardship, consent, accountability, and sovereignty
